cards.front: What is the primary goal of Similarity-based Learning?
cards.back: To identify patterns and relationships between data points
A type of Similarity-based Learning that classifies new data points by finding the most similar existing data point

cards.front: What is the Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor (WKNN) Algorithm?
cards.back: An extension of K-Nearest-Neighbor Learning that assigns weights to the k most similar data points based on their distance

cards.front: What is the advantage of using WKNN over traditional KNN?
cards.back: WKNN can handle noisy or missing data points, as it assigns higher weights to more similar data points

cards.front: What is the disadvantage of using WKNN?
cards.back: WKNN can be computationally expensive, as it requires calculating the distance between each data point and the target data point for each of the k nearest neighbors
